weather_report:
Late morning in Herdsman arrives in a pewter wash, the sun a shy rumor behind high cloud. The UV will be modest at best, perhaps a brief nudge around midday if the ceiling thins, then slipping lower as light showers drift in after lunch and linger toward late afternoon. Expect only fleeting glimmers of brightness; the day wears a matte finish, distance slightly muted and edges softened by the damp.

It’s a gently cool, serviceable sort of day—pleasant if you dress for it. A light waterproof over breathable layers, sturdy shoes with grip, and a small umbrella will suit; sunglasses optional, sunscreen minimal. If you have errands, use the late morning while the sky holds; later, take short, unhurried walks between showers, settle by a café window, or do the kind of homely tasks that pair well with gray light—tidying a desk, tending plants, putting a simple soup on. Evening looks damp and reflective, the kind that makes streetlights bloom softly on wet pavement.
